event: 12-lead electrocardiogram demonstrated inferior ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction
transferred to a tertiary care hospital
coronary angiography demonstrated occlusion of the distal left-dominant posterior descending artery
transthoracic echocardiography showed left ventricular apical-inferior hypokinesis
cranial computed tomography identified a small cerebellar hemorrhage
transferred to cardiac intensive care unit
onset of polymorphic ventricular tachycardia storm
admission blood cultures grew methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus
multifocal suppuration of the lower interventricular septum
posterior descending artery exhibited acute bacterial vasculitis
